title: NPC Schedule (archetype extension)
description: Schema fragment describing the `schedule_template:` field added to records in an existing archetypes.yaml. Per PRD §1.3 NPC schedules are NOT a new top-level YAML file — they embed inside archetype definitions so that an archetype carries its routine alongside its name/stats/disposition. The Phase-2 NPC schedule generator resolves where named NPCs are at a given time of day by looking up their archetype's schedule_template and translating template locations into world-specific places. This schema describes only the `schedule_template:` field; the surrounding archetype object lives elsewhere.

schedule_template.description: Map of role id (open enum: 'blacksmith', 'merchant', 'innkeeper', 'fixer', 'station_chief', ...) to the role's daily routine. An archetype embeds one entry per role; an NPC whose archetype matches a role inherits that routine.

dailyRoutine.description: Time-of-day slots. The slot ids MUST match the world's calendar.yaml `time_precision` (e.g. `quarter_day` ⇒ 'dawn', 'midday', 'dusk', 'night'). At least one slot is required, but absent slots are permitted — the generator falls back to the nearest defined slot.

location.description: Where the NPC is. Open enum — values can be archetypal ('forge', 'tavern', 'home') and are resolved to world-specific places at runtime by the schedule generator (24-era follow-on) using world demographics.

activity.description: What the NPC is doing. Short verb phrase the narrator may surface verbatim ('stoking the fire', 'serving patrons').

available.description: Optional. Defaults to true. If false, the NPC is in-slot but unavailable to players (sleeping, in private audience, etc.). The narrator says where they are; players still cannot interact.

dusk.activity: behind the bar, chatting with regulars
